episode_title: Crunch Time in the Dev Team

scene.setting: The office, in the middle of a coding sprint.
scene.time: 5:45 PM on a Friday, just before the weekly sprint review.
scene.context: The team has been struggling to meet their sprint goals, and tensions are running high. The CTO has announced a surprise call with a potential investor next Monday, and the team needs to deliver a polished demo by then.

antagonist_opening_line: Rohan, I reviewed your code commits, and I'm worried that we're not making enough progress on the critical path. Can you explain why you thought it was a good idea to refactor the database schema this late in the sprint?

strategy_chips.label: Own the Problem
strategy_chips.philosophy: Taking full accountability for the issue shows that you're proactive and committed to finding a solution.

strategy_chips.label: Redirect to Root Cause
strategy_chips.philosophy: Focusing on the underlying causes of the problem shows that you're strategic and willing to invest time in solving the root issue.

strategy_chips.label: Reframe the Challenge
strategy_chips.philosophy: Reframing the challenge as an opportunity to learn and improve shows that you're adaptable and open to new ideas.

success_criteria: Successfully diffused the tension with Rohan and refocused the team on meeting the sprint goals.
Identified and addressed the root cause of the issue, preventing similar problems in the future.
Developed a plan to present the demo to the investor, highlighting the team's strengths and accomplishments.
transfer_targets: effectively resolving conflicts with team members in a high-pressure situation
identifying and addressing the root cause of a technical issue
presenting a clear and compelling demo to stakeholders
